page: Amazon Web Services r5a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 virtual CPUs across 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The dedicated CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz and includes an L3 cache of 8 MB. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear tradeoff between memory capacity and raw computational speed. The instance demonstrates average performance in multi-core Geekbench tests, software compilation, and Passmark CPU evaluations, including encryption and physics simulations. However, single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10% according to stress-ng and Geekbench single-core metrics. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is average, but large-block uncached read and write operations fall into the weak and poor categories respectively. Redis SET operations and text generation for small language models achieve average throughput, while static web serving and larger LLM inference tasks show weaker performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5a.4xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-frequency single-core processing or local NVMe storage. The instance is suited for database operations, caching layers such as Redis, and memory-bound enterprise applications. It is also appropriate for moderate multi-threaded tasks like software compilation and image processing, where the 128.0 GB DDR4 memory footprint prevents out-of-memory bottlenecks.
description: Amazon Web Services r5a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz, this x86_64 instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 5 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks show average results in multi-core operations, software compilation, and database tasks, though single-core compute and large-block memory write speeds are relatively weak. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive applications that do not demand high single-thread performance. It is suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, designed for database operations and memory-bound workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated AMD EPYC processors for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with dedicated CPU allocation
Average performance in database operations, Redis caching, and multi-core benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for memory-bound applications, database hosting, and caching layers
